channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Mamdami Demands U.S. Immigration Policy Start Obeying Islam
item.description: Mamdani invoked Islamic doctrine to define civic obligation and delegitimize lawful, constitutional authority.

item.title: Here Are 10 Great Justice Scalia Quotes To Mark A Decade Since His Passing
item.description: Here are some of Justice Scalia's best moments in remembrance of the 10-year anniversary of his passing.

item.title: 'Media tribalism and media bias': Trump's flurry of lawsuits changes the game for news outlets
item.description: President Trump has buried media outlets in an unprecedented avalanche of lawsuits alleging they defamed him and sullied his reputation, a move legal ...

item.title: Trump’s FTC chief says he’s not the ‘speech police,’ but reality has too liberal a bias on Apple News app
item.description: Andrew Ferguson urged Cook to review what is used on the Apple News feed and take corrective action.

item.title: Politicians scramble on data centers after putting their voters on the hook for Big Tech’s job-killing AI efforts
item.description: “What do you do when Big Tech, because of the very profitable nature of these data centers, can simply outbid grandma for power in the short run?”

item.title: Wuthering Lows: A Grossly Sexualized Brontë Adaptation
item.description: Emerald Fennell continues her soft-core feminism.

item.title: As Trump touts tariff windfall, battleground states shoulder billions in costs
item.description: Swing states face massive tariff costs as midterm elections approach, with affordability concerns dominating voter priorities in contested races.

item.title: Gen Z Is ‘Dumber’ Thanks To Adults Saturating Their Lives With Screens
item.description: Children can't be expected to dig deeper in an educational system engineered to keep them skimming.

item.title: Eileen Gu’s Terrible Choice
item.description: Gu skis for China, a choice that is a little like deciding to represent a fascist country during the 1930s.

item.title: Republicans Will Keep Losing Until They Realize Power Isn’t Evil
item.description: We can exercise power, or we can allow our enemies to exercise it. Choose wisely.

item.title: Trump DOJ files new lawsuit accusing Harvard of withholding records on race in admissions
Fetterman bucks Democrats, says party put politics over country in DHS shutdown standoff
item.description: A DHS partial shutdown is set for midnight Friday after Senate Democrats blocked funding over ICE reforms, though immigration operations will continue...

item.title: Congress Has Always Held the Power over Tariffs
item.description: The House has finally rebuked President Trump over his unilateral trade war.

item.title: The Week: The Nancy Guthrie Disappearance
item.description: Plus: Trump rolls back climate regulations.

item.title: You love your dog too much. Blame the broken American Dream and loss of purpose since the pandemic
item.description: Dogs aren’t just being used as a substitute for people. I believe Americans are turning to dogs to alleviate the erosion of social life itself.

item.title: Turns Out The Irishman Detained By ICE Was Facing Drug Charges Back Home
item.description: The arrest of Irishman Seamus Culleton fueled a false media narrative that ICE is grabbing law-abiding people who have done nothing wrong.

item.title: 'The old world is gone': Rubio looks to reexamine relationship with allies at Munich conference
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who is attending the Munich Security Conference, could have an opportunity to clearly define Washington's relationship...

item.title: ‘Save China and Save America’: The Road Not Taken
item.description: A century-old correspondence portends the challenges the U.S. still faces in Asia.

item.title: Valentine’s Day office etiquette: Experts share the 80:20 rule for talking about your significant other at work—and the 3 times when it’s never okay
item.description: While Hallmark may want you to confess your undying love for your partner, perhaps these declarations are best left inside a card—and well away from t...

item.title: Government to shut down at midnight after Dems, White House fail to strike DHS deal
item.description: Government shutdown of Department of Homeland Security appears all but guaranteed by midnight as Senate Republicans and Democrats engage in a blame ga...

item.title: Trump is making foreign tourism great again. How much will he hurt the World Cup?
item.description: While the rest of the world saw a travel bump in 2025, with global international arrivals up 4%, the U.S. saw a downturn

item.title: Republican attorneys general demand DOJ investigate foreign funding tied to 150 climate groups in US
item.description: Nineteen Republican attorneys general asked the DOJ to investigate U.S. nonprofits that received nearly $2 billion in foreign climate funding for pote...

item.title: Palestinian protester, detained for nearly a year, says 'inhumane' jail conditions prompted seizure
item.description: A Palestinian woman who has been held in an immigration jail for nearly a year after she attended a protest in New York City said she suffered a seizu...

item.title: State Department links left-leaning nonprofits to Chinese propaganda
item.description: The State Department told Congress this week that left-leaning nonprofits Code Pink and the People's Forum are linked to Chinese influence operations.

item.title: Arizona GOP candidate drops out of primary, narrowing field to two
item.description: Housing developer Karrin Taylor Robson has suspended her campaign for Arizona governor, saying Thursday she doesn't want a divisive GOP primary that w...

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Bank Risk
item.description: AI is exposing the limits of legacy bank controls, making governance quality critical to resilience and investor confidence. The post AI Is Reshaping ...

item.title: What Makes an Ideal Leveraged Buyout Candidate?
item.description: In competitive PE markets, successful LBOs depend on durable business models, recurring cash flows, defensible market positions, and disciplined targe...

item.title: Rethinking Household Asset Allocation Under Capital Constraints
item.description: Leveraged ETFs aren’t about more risk, they’re about using less capital to achieve a desired risk profile in household portfolios. The post Rethinking...

item.title: Stockholm’s Capital Markets Success: More Than Meatballs
item.description: Stockholm’s capital-market success reflects more than IPOs and PE activity. It offers a case study in how culture, institutions, and incentives shape ...
